free_text: A Study of the Cr52(d,p)Cr53 Reaction
The 52Cr targets were self-supporting and made from
Silicon surface barrier were used to detect the
protons from the (d,p) reaction and a thin solid-state
counter used to identify the particles thus eliminating
the background due to elastic deuteron scattering.
The errors in the measurements of the
differential cross-sections were almost entirely due
to counting statistics except in the cases where there
was overlapping of the proton peaks. Where this
occurred, as in the case of the 3.61 MeV and 3.70 MeV
states, the biggest error was the estimated error in
separating the contribution to each proton group from
the region of overlap. Estimates of background contri-
butions in the spectra gave values that were much
smaller than the errors due to counting statistics.
